perturbation.original_text: University of Arkansas at Little Rock STANDARD NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT This Agreement is effective (____ /_____ /______) between ____ _______________ (hereinafter “ Recipient ”) with its principal place of business at this address _____________________ _____________________ and the Board of Trustees of the University of Arkansas acting for and on behalf of the University of Arkansas at Little Rock (hereinafter “University”), a not -for-profit educational institution formed under the laws of the State of Arkansas, with offices at _ 2801 S. University Ave, Little Rock AR, 72204 . The parties agree as follows:
perturbation.changed_text: University of Arkansas at Little Rock STANDARD NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT This Agreement is effective (____ /_____ /______) between ____ _______________ (hereinafter “Client”) with its principal place of business at this address _____________________ _____________________ and the Board of Trustees of the University of Arkansas acting for and on behalf of the University of Arkansas at Little Rock (hereinafter “University”), a not -for-profit educational institution formed under the laws of the State of Arkansas, with offices at _ 2801 S. University Ave, Little Rock AR, 72204 . The parties agree as follows:
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Recipient' is replaced with 'Client' at the beginning of the agreement. This introduces ambiguity because the rest of the document uses 'Recipient', and whether 'Client' and 'Recipient' refer to the same entity is not explicitly defined, leading to potential confusion about obligations and rights throughout the document. This is particularly an in-text contradiction because the definition of 'Recipient' (as defined in the original term) is being contradicted at the very start, however, it will continue to be used throughout the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Unless expressly authorized in writing by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party agrees to retain the Confidential Information in confidence and shall not copy or disclose the Confidential Information to or use the Confidenti al Information for the benefit of any third party. Confidential Information shall only be disclosed to the Receiving Party’s employees and, even then, only to the extent that such employees have a specific need to know of the Confidential information, for the evaluation of the proposed transaction. Before receiving any part of the Confidential Information, Receiving Party’s employees shall be required to read this Non -disclosure Agreement and, by receiving such Confidential Information, such employee shall acknowledge and agree to abide by the Receiving Party’s obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces "Recipient" with "Client" at the beginning of the agreement, while the contradicted text uses "Receiving Party" (which is implied to be the same as 'Recipient'). This creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement now uses two different terms ('Client' and 'Receiving Party') to refer to the same entity, leading to ambiguity and uncertainty about obligations, as described in the Perturbation Explanation and aligning with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Article 1. Confidential Information is defined as any information, whether written or verbal, of either party hereto, (Disclosing Party) which is disclosed to o r observed by the other party (Receiving Party) in connection with or as a result of the evaluation of any possible transaction between the Univers ity of Arkansas at Little Rock and ______________________________ and which is, at the time of disclosure, marked as being Confident ial or Proprietary, or is reasonably identifiable as confidential, proprietary information of the Disclosing Party pertaining to information in the areas of (subject matter) : _______________________________________________ ___________________________. Such Confidential Information may include, but is not li mited to, business plans, forecasts, content, processes, projections or analysis, software, hardware, product, or system designs, specifications, documentation, code, structure, or protocols. Confidential Information that is disclosed verbally will also be included as proprietary.
perturbation.changed_text: Article 1. Confidential Information is defined as any data, whether written or verbal, of either party hereto, (Disclosing Party) which is disclosed to o r observed by the other party (Receiving Party) in connection with or as a result of the evaluation of any possible transaction between the Univers ity of Arkansas at Little Rock and ______________________________ and which is, at the time of disclosure, marked as being Confident ial or Proprietary, or is reasonably identifiable as confidential, proprietary information of the Disclosing Party pertaining to information in the areas of (subject matter) : _______________________________________________ ___________________________. Such Confidential Information may include, but is not li mited to, business plans, forecasts, content, processes, projections or analysis, software, hardware, product, or system designs, specifications, documentation, code, structure, or protocols. Confidential Information that is disclosed verbally will also be included as proprietary.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'information' to 'data' in the definition of 'Confidential Information'. While seemingly minor, it creates ambiguity. 'Data' typically refers to structured, often digital, information. If the Disclosing Party shares non-digital or unstructured information (e.g., a verbal strategy discussion), it might be argued that it's not covered by the definition of 'Confidential Information' because it doesn't qualify as 'data' as well as this term appears in Article 6, leading to an in-text contradiction with how the contract intends to protect confidential information beyond just digital data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No license, express or implied, in the Confidential Information is granted to either party other than to use the information in the manner and to the extent authorized by this Agreement. Each Party shall retain the t itle and full ownership rights to their respective “Confidential Information”.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'information' to 'data' narrows the scope of what is considered 'Confidential Information.' While the original text covered any type of information (written or verbal), the changed text focuses on 'data.' The explanation suggests that the modified definition may exclude verbal strategy discussions or unstructured information from being considered confidential, which contradicts the Contradicted Text's intention to protect all 'Confidential Information,' regardless of its form. This creates an inconsistency in how Confidential Information is defined and protected within the contract. Given 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, since the contract now limits 'confidential information' to data, this means that Article 6, which grants ownership to 'confidential information', is limited and thus this causes a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Unless expressly authorized in writing by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party agrees to retain the Confidential Information in confidence and shall not copy or disclose the Confidential Information to or use the Confidenti al Information for the benefit of any third party. Confidential Information shall only be disclosed to the Receiving Party’s employees and, even then, only to the extent that such employees have a specific need to know of the Confidential information, for the evaluation of the proposed transaction. Before receiving any part of the Confidential Information, Receiving Party’s employees shall be required to read this Non -disclosure Agreement and, by receiving such Confidential Information, such employee shall acknowledge and agree to abide by the Receiving Party’s obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless expressly authorized in writing by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party agrees to retain the Confidential Materials in confidence and shall not copy or disclose the Confidential Materials to or use the Confidenti al Materials for the benefit of any third party. Confidential Information shall only be disclosed to the Receiving Party’s employees and, even then, only to the extent that such employees have a specific need to know of the Confidential information, for the evaluation of the proposed transaction. Before receiving any part of the Confidential Information, Receiving Party’s employees shall be required to read this Non -disclosure Agreement and, by receiving such Confidential Information, such employee shall acknowledge and agree to abide by the Receiving Party’s obligations hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Materials' in the first sentence of Article 2, but not consistently throughout the paragraph. This creates confusion. Does 'Confidential Materials' have a different meaning than 'Confidential Information'? Are the Receiving Party's obligations different with respect to 'Materials' versus 'Information'? The definition in Article 1, which defines 'Confidential Information' as information that might include software. However, there is no definition for 'Confidential Materials', which contradicts the original intent in Article 1, because the term is being used interchangeably without a definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information is defined as any information, whether written or verbal, of either party hereto, (Disclosing Party) which is disclosed to o r observed by the other party (Receiving Party) in connection with or as a result of the evaluation of any possible transaction between the Univers ity of Arkansas at Little Rock and ______________________________ and which is, at the time of disclosure, marked as being Confident ial or Proprietary, or is reasonably identifiable as confidential, proprietary information of the Disclosing Party pertaining to information in the areas of (subject matter) : _______________________________________________ ___________________________. Such Confidential Information may include, but is not li mited to, business plans, forecasts, content, processes, projections or analysis, software, hardware, product, or system designs, specifications, documentation, code, structure, or protocols. Confidential Information that is disclosed verbally will also be included as proprietary.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Confidential Materials' in the first sentence of Article 2, while the rest of the paragraph still uses 'Confidential Information'. The definition in the 'Contradicted Text' explicitly defines 'Confidential Information', but 'Confidential Materials' remains undefined. This creates a contradiction because the receiving party's obligations are unclear: do they pertain to both 'Information' and 'Materials,' or only 'Information'? If the former, the lack of definition of 'Materials' introduces ambiguity and if the latter, the changed first sentence is inconsistent with the rest of the paragraph and with the defined 'Confidential Information' in the 'Contradicted Text'. The Misaligned Terminology results in an in-text contradiction as per the definitions.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding any other provisions of the Agreement, each party acknowledges that Confidential Information shall not include any information which: a. was known to the Receivi ng Party prior to the disclosure hereunder; b. was received from a third party not under an obligation of confidence to Receiving Party; c. is in the public domain at the time of disclosure hereunder or subsequently entered in the public domain without the fault of the Receiving Party; d. has been independently developed by an employee of the Receiving Party that has not had access directly or indirectly to Confidential Information, and Receiving Party can substantiate any claim of independent development by writt en evidence; or e. is required to be disclosed by law, provided however that the Receiving Party shall give immediate notice of any such request for disclosure and cooperate with the Disclosing Party in its efforts to obtain a protective order or other protec tion from the requirement or consequences of disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding any other provisions of the Agreement, each party acknowledges that Confidential Data shall not include any information which: a. was known to the Receivi ng Party prior to the disclosure hereunder; b. was received from a third party not under an obligation of confidence to Receiving Party; c. is in the public domain at the time of disclosure hereunder or subsequently entered in the public domain without the fault of the Receiving Party; d. has been independently developed by an employee of the Receiving Party that has not had access directly or indirectly to Confidential Information, and Receiving Party can substantiate any claim of independent development by writt en evidence; or e. is required to be disclosed by law, provided however that the Receiving Party shall give immediate notice of any such request for disclosure and cooperate with the Disclosing Party in its efforts to obtain a protective order or other protec tion from the requirement or consequences of disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: Change 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Data'. Now the agreement says that 'Confidential Data shall not include any information', creating a logical contradiction. If something is not included as 'Confidential Data', does it still fall under the protection of the agreement, since the agreement is largely about protecting 'Confidential Information', this causes confusion about the scope of the agreement and introduces an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information is defined as any information, whether written or verbal, of either party hereto, (Disclosing Party) which is disclosed to o r observed by the other party (Receiving Party) in connection with or as a result of the evaluation of any possible transaction between the Univers ity of Arkansas at Little Rock and ______________________________ and which is, at the time of disclosure, marked as being Confident ial or Proprietary, or is reasonably identifiable as confidential, proprietary information of the Disclosing Party pertaining to information in the areas of (subject matter) : _______________________________________________ ___________________________. Such Confidential Information may include, but is not li mited to, business plans, forecasts, content, processes, projections or analysis, software, hardware, product, or system designs, specifications, documentation, code, structure, or protocols. Confidential Information that is disclosed verbally will also be included as proprietary.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Confidential Information' throughout the agreement. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Confidential Data' in one section. The contradicted text defines 'Confidential Information'. This creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement now refers to two different terms ('Confidential Information' and 'Confidential Data') without clarifying their relationship, leading to uncertainty about which information is protected and causing a direct conflict with the contradicted section that defines only 'Confidential Information'. Because of this redefinition, the agreement becomes ambiguous as it is not clear whether the changed text is referring to data as a subset, superset, or something entirely different from 'information'. This falls under the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party shall retain the t itle and full ownership rights to their respective “Confidential Information”.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party shall retain the t itle and full ownership rights to their respective “Proprietary Data”.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Data' in the last sentence of Article 6. This implies that there is another term 'Proprietary Data'. Does 'Proprietary Data' include all 'Confidential Information' or vice versa? It introduces inconsistency that creates an in-text contradiction and potential disputes over ownership rights. It also undermines the definition provided in Article 1, where 'Confidential Information' is properly and fully defined, and 'Proprietary Data' isn't defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information is defined as any information, whether written or verbal, of either party hereto, (Disclosing Party) which is disclosed to o r observed by the other party (Receiving Party) in connection with or as a result of the evaluation of any possible transaction between the Univers ity of Arkansas at Little Rock and ______________________________ and which is, at the time of disclosure, marked as being Confident ial or Proprietary, or is reasonably identifiable as confidential, proprietary information of the Disclosing Party pertaining to information in the areas of (subject matter) : _______________________________________________ ___________________________. Such Confidential Information may include, but is not li mited to, business plans, forecasts, content, processes, projections or analysis, software, hardware, product, or system designs, specifications, documentation, code, structure, or protocols. Confidential Information that is disclosed verbally will also be included as proprietary.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Data' introduces a term not previously defined in the contract, contradicting the existing definition and usage of 'Confidential Information' in the 'Contradicted Text'. The 'Contradicted Text' provides a specific definition of 'Confidential Information', and by introducing a new, undefined term, the changed text creates ambiguity and potential conflict regarding which information is covered by the confidentiality obligations. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition that specifies using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them leads to conflicting obligations. This directly impacts the clarity and enforceability of the confidentiality clause, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Non -disclosure Agreement is effective on the date specified in the Caption of the Agreement and will remain in effect for a period of five (5) years.
perturbation.changed_text: This Confidentiality Agreement is effective on the date specified in the Caption of the Agreement and will remain in effect for a period of three (3) years.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Non-disclosure Agreement' to 'Confidentiality Agreement' and changing the duration from five (5) years to three (3) years. Creates ambiguity about what agreement this is. The title is a 'Non-disclosure Agreement,' but is referred to here as a 'Confidentiality Agreement.' A confidentiality agreement and Non-disclosure agreement have their own specific scope, thus there is a contradiction in the type of agreement, thus making this an in-text contradiction. The reduced duration introduces conflict with the overall agreement. If this change goes unnoticed the disclosing party will be operating on 2 different agreements, with 2 different lengths of time.
perturbation.contradicted_text: University of Arkansas at Little Rock STANDARD NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT This Agreement is effective (____ /_____ /______) between ____ _______________ (hereinafter “Client”) with its principal place of business at this address _____________________ _____________________ and the Board of Trustees of the University of Arkansas acting for and on behalf of the University of Arkansas at Little Rock (hereinafter “University”), a not -for-profit educational institution formed under the laws of the State of Arkansas, with offices at _ 2801 S. University Ave, Little Rock AR, 72204 . The parties agree as follows:
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'Non-disclosure Agreement' to 'Confidentiality Agreement' and changes the duration from five (5) years to three (3) years. The contradicted text specifically references 'University of Arkansas at Little Rock STANDARD NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT.' The changed text, by referring to the agreement as a 'Confidentiality Agreement' and changing the duration, introduces a contradiction as it no longer aligns with the explicitly stated 'NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT' in the contradicted text. Additionally, reducing the duration creates a timing conflict, suggesting different lengths of commitment for the same agreement, therefore meeting the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.
